Off Calendar Mar-12-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
David Annicchiarico, Esq., appearing for plaintiff and moving party: SHANNON ZIEMBA.
Adrian G. Driscoll, Esq., appearing for defendant and opposing party: STATIONARY ENGINEERS APPRENTICE TRAINING TRUST AND JIM MAPLE

HEARING HELD AND THE TENTATIVE RULING IS ADOPTED WITH MODIFICATION. Plaintiff's motion to quash or modifiy is granted in part. As to the medical records subpoenas, the subpoenas are modified to only require production of records from January 1, 2001 to the present by 3/16/07. This order is without prejudice to defendant seeking leave of Court to issue further subpoenas for records before said date upon a showing of good cause. The motion is denied as to the employment records subpoenas.

MODIFICATION: To provide that medical records from 1/01/01 shall be produced by 3/16/07. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Deft International Union Of Operating Engineers Local 39'S Further Responses To Pltf'S Form Interrogatories - Employment Law (Set One) And Request For Production Of Documents (Set One)
Off Calendar Nov-28-2006 Continued To Dec-13-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Pltf'S Further Responses To Stationary Engineering Apprentice Training Trust'S Form Interrogatories - Employment Law, Set 1;
Off Calendar Nov-02-2006 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Andrea Laiacona for defendant and moving party: International Uion of Operating Engineers
Emily e. Arnold-Fernandez for plaintiff and opposing party: Shannon Ziemba

HEARING HELD and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant International Union of Operating Engineers' motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories
200.3, 200.4
The objections are overruled and stricken. If plaintiff lacks sufficient knowledge to respond fully, the response shall comply with CCP 2030.220(c).
204.2, 210.2, 210.3, 212.2, 212.3, 212.4, 212.5, 212.6, 212.7, 213.1, 213.2,
Granted. The objections are overruled and stricken. It is not a proper objection that an interrogatory is premature as discovery is ongoing. Plaintiff is ordered to serve supplemental responses that comply with CCP 2030.220(c) within 15 days of notice of this order.
215.1
Denied
215.2
Granted in part. Plaintiff is ordered to serve a supplemental response specifying whether any statements were obtained by counsel or were independently prepared by witnesses. Statements written or recorded independently by witnesses are neither absolute nor qualified work product.
The Court finds that plaintiff asserted unmeritorious objections. However, the Court declines to award monetary sanctions at this time. Compliance by November 20, 20006. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN
